title: rosa communis var. oxyphylla
intro: Rosa communis var. oxyphylla, also known as the wild briar or European dog rose, is a perennial shrub that belongs to the rose family. This plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly its anti-inflammatory and astringent effects, which have been shown to promote wound healing and reduce swelling. Rosa communis var. oxyphylla is also valued for its health benefits, including its ability to alleviate digestive issues, reduce fever, and support the immune system. The plant can be prepared as an herbal tea, tincture, or salve, and is often used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
